symptoms.symptom_name: Slowly enlarging painless nodule on the nose over two years, starting as a small pearly spot

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Indolent multi-year growth of a pearly nodule is characteristic of BCC and argues strongly against a rapidly-erupting benign lesion.
Pyogenic granulomas erupt and grow over weeks, not years — the chronicity effectively excludes it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pearly translucent nodule with a rolled border, arborising surface telangiectasias and central ulceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The pearly rolled border with arborising telangiectasias over a central ('rodent') ulcer is the classic macroscopic signature of nodular BCC; visible on close video though dermoscopy/biopsy is confirmatory.
SCC is typically hyperkeratotic, crusted and inflamed rather than pearly and translucent.

symptoms.symptom_name: Easy recurrent contact bleeding, now a continuous slow venous ooze from the central ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fragile tumour neovasculature bleeds with trivial trauma; a bleeding, ulcerating lesion also raises the malignancy concern that mandates biopsy.
BCC is characteristically painless and non-pruritic; absence of pain/itch argues against an inflammatory or infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable — normal blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate and oxygen saturation (nurse-relayed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: basal cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations indicate no significant blood loss or shock — the patient-obtainable data that justifies urgent-but-not-emergency triage rather than a 000 call.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: basal cell carcinoma with acute haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Antiplatelet therapy prolongs bleeding from the fragile tumour surface, explaining the persistent ooze and informing hemostasis and follow-up.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lesion on chronically sun-exposed facial skin (nasal ala)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cumulative UV exposure at a sun-exposed facial site is the dominant risk factor for BCC; the nasal-ala site also carries higher recurrence risk requiring specialist excision planning.

symptoms.symptom_name: No surrounding induration, spreading erythema, warmth, purulence or fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpation and inspection for induration/spread are clinician-only; their absence excludes an acute soft-tissue infection and argues against antibiotics.
Absence of regional nodes is reassuring against metastatic spread; requires clinician palpation and so is sealed as answer-key, not obtainable in chat.
Histology is the only way to confirm BCC and definitively exclude SCC and melanoma; unavailable in telehealth and the reason a referral pathway is mandatory.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin biopsy (punch or excisional) with histopathology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive tissue diagnosis and to guide excision; the core reason an in-person skin-cancer referral is required. Cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Dermoscopy / in-person lesion examination by a skin clinician
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the lesion and plans management; requires in-person assessment.
Not indicated for a controllable local bleed in a stable patient on low-dose aspirin; ordering broad bloods here is unnecessary over-investigation.
